user_message: Just came out of deployment blockers with Adatum Corporation. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-03-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Adatum Corporation)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:08:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:12:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:15:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:23:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-O -- Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
